title: A library I miss visiting
summary: Describe a library you used to visit and explain why you miss it.
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a library you used to visit and now miss. Describe what it looked like and what you usually did there. Use past simple and comparatives where appropriate. Explain why you no longer go there and why you miss it.
hintStarters: When I was living in …, I used to visit a library that…
The library had a reading room where I always…
I miss that library because it was a place where I could…
sampleText: When I was a teenager living in Kraków, I used to visit a public library near the old town square almost every week. The building was over a hundred years old and had high ceilings, large windows, and long rows of dark wooden shelves that reached up to the ceiling. I loved the way the morning light came through the tall windows and fell across the reading tables. The library had a hushed, calm atmosphere that was unlike anywhere else in the city. I usually went on Saturday mornings, chose two or three books, and sat at the same table near the window for a couple of hours. The librarian, a woman with short grey hair and round glasses, knew which section I liked and sometimes pointed me towards new arrivals. I borrowed novels mostly, but sometimes I stayed just to browse without taking anything home. I moved away from Kraków when I was eighteen to study in another city, and the library closed down two years after I left. I found out from a friend who told me the building became a café. I felt sad when I heard that, because that library was one of those quiet places where I could think clearly and feel completely calm. No café can replace the particular silence of a room full of books.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: long rows of dark wooden shelves that reached up to the ceiling
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: những hàng kệ gỗ tối màu dài chạy lên tận trần nhà

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The way the morning light came through the tall windows
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Cách ánh sáng buổi sáng chiếu qua những cửa sổ cao
bầu không khí yên tĩnh, bình thản

sampleAnnotations.phrase: unlike anywhere else in the city
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: không giống bất kỳ nơi nào khác trong thành phố

sampleAnnotations.phrase: sometimes pointed me towards new arrivals
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đôi khi chỉ cho tôi xem sách mới về
ở lại chỉ để lướt xem sách
tòa nhà trở thành một quán cà phê

sampleAnnotations.phrase: one of those quiet places where I could think clearly
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một trong những nơi yên tĩnh nơi tôi có thể suy nghĩ rõ ràng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the particular silence of a room full of books
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: sự im lặng đặc biệt của một căn phòng đầy sách

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Used to + base verb for past habits
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'used to + base verb' để nói về những thói quen trong quá khứ không còn xảy ra nữa: 'I used to visit a library almost every week.' Cấu trúc này ngụ ý rằng bạn không còn làm điều đó nữa.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'used to + base verb' to describe past habits that no longer happen: 'I used to visit a library almost every week.' This structure implies you no longer do it.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi còn là thiếu niên sống ở Kraków, tôi thường đến một thư viện công cộng gần quảng trường phố cổ gần như mỗi tuần. Tòa nhà hơn một trăm tuổi, có trần nhà cao, cửa sổ lớn và những hàng kệ gỗ tối dài chạy lên tận trần. Tôi yêu cách ánh sáng buổi sáng chiếu qua những cửa sổ cao và đổ xuống các bàn đọc. Thư viện có bầu không khí yên tĩnh, bình thản không giống bất kỳ nơi nào khác trong thành phố. Tôi thường đến vào sáng thứ Bảy, chọn hai ba cuốn sách và ngồi ở cùng một chiếc bàn gần cửa sổ vài tiếng. Thủ thư, một người phụ nữ tóc xám ngắn và đeo kính tròn, biết khu vực tôi thích và đôi khi chỉ cho tôi xem sách mới về. Tôi chủ yếu mượn tiểu thuyết, nhưng đôi khi chỉ ở lại để lướt xem mà không mang gì về nhà. Tôi rời Kraków năm mười tám tuổi để học ở thành phố khác và thư viện đóng cửa hai năm sau khi tôi đi. Tôi biết điều đó từ một người bạn kể rằng tòa nhà trở thành một quán cà phê. Tôi cảm thấy buồn khi nghe tin đó, vì thư viện đó là một trong những nơi yên tĩnh nơi tôi có thể suy nghĩ rõ ràng và cảm thấy hoàn toàn bình tâm. Không có quán cà phê nào có thể thay thế sự im lặng đặc biệt của một căn phòng đầy sách.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where was the library the writer describes?
options: Near the old town square in Kraków
In a university building in Warsaw
In the city centre of Prague
Near the writer's school
mcQuestions.explanation: The library was near the old town square in Kraków.

options: I used to visit a library almost every week.
I used to visiting a library almost every week.
I use to visit a library almost every week.
I was used to visit a library almost every week.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Used to + base verb' is the correct form for past habits.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Used to' is always followed by the base form of the verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did the writer usually do at the library?
options: Sat near the window and read for a couple of hours
Studied for school exams
Met friends to chat quietly
Used the computers to do research
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer chose books and sat at the same table near the window for a couple of hours.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentence about the writer's typical Saturday routine.

options: Very quiet and calm
Very dark
Very old
Very cold
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Hushed' means very quiet, often because people are speaking softly or not at all.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about the sound level in a library.

mcQuestions.prompt: What happened to the library after the writer left Kraków?
options: It closed down and became a café
It moved to a different building
It was rebuilt and made larger
It was turned into a school
mcQuestions.explanation: The library closed down and the building became a café.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentence about what happened to the building.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the librarian do when she sees 'new arrivals'?
options: Points the writer towards newly received books
Shows the writer where the exits are
Tells the writer the library is closing
Asks the writer to return overdue books
mcQuestions.explanation: The librarian pointed the writer towards new arrivals — newly received books in the writer's preferred section.
mcQuestions.hint: In a library, 'new arrivals' refers to books that have just been added.

options: To look through books without a specific goal
To borrow a book
To read a book from start to finish
To look up a word in a dictionary
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Browse' means to look at things in a relaxed, unhurried way without a specific goal.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about looking through books on a shelf without a clear plan.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct comparative sentence from the passage.
options: a hushed, calm atmosphere that was unlike anywhere else in the city
a hushed, calm atmosphere that was more unlike anywhere else in the city
a hushed, calm atmosphere that was the most unlike anywhere in the city
a hushed, calm atmosphere that was unlike any other in the world
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Unlike anywhere else' is an idiomatic comparison meaning there was no other place the same.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the exact phrase used in the passage for the unique atmosphere.

options: To permanently stop operating
To shut the doors at the end of each day
To reduce opening hours
To move to a new location
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Close down' means to permanently stop operating and cease to exist as a business or institution.
mcQuestions.hint: This is different from closing at the end of the day.

options: Light from the sun coming through windows
Soft electric lighting
Candlelight
Light from lamps on shelves
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Natural light' refers to sunlight that enters a space, as opposed to artificial electric light.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about where light comes from if there are no electric lights on.

mcQuestions.prompt: What is the writer's main reason for missing the library?
options: It was a place to think clearly and feel calm
It had the best book collection in the city
The librarian was very helpful
It was close to the writer's home
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer says it was a place 'where I could think clearly and feel completely calm.'
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentence that starts with 'because that library was...'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do you think public libraries are still important today, when people can find most information online? What do libraries offer that the internet cannot?
